purpose.summary: This copybook defines the structure PASFLPCB, which appears to be related to IMS database Program Communication Blocks (PCBs). It contains fields for database name, segment level, PCB status, processing options, segment name, key feedback length, number of sensitive segments, and key feedback area.

paragraphs.purpose: This copybook defines the data structure PASFLPCB, which is likely used to represent an IMS PCB (Program Communication Block). The structure contains information about the database, segment, and key feedback area. PASFL-DBDNAME (line 18) stores the name of the database. PASFL-SEG-LEVEL (line 19) indicates the segment level. PASFL-PCB-STATUS (line 20) provides the status of the PCB. PASFL-PCB-PROCOPT (line 21) specifies the processing options. PASFL-SEG-NAME (line 23) stores the name of the segment. PASFL-KEYFB-NAME (line 24) and PASFL-NUM-SENSEGS (line 25) define the key feedback length and number of sensitive segments, respectively. PASFL-KEYFB (line 26) is a 100-byte field that likely holds the key feedback area. This copybook is used to define the structure in WORKING-STORAGE or LINKAGE SECTION of COBOL programs that interact with IMS databases.
paragraphs.dead_code_reason: Record layout 'PASFLPCB' is never used by any program
